research_conducted.finding: jscodeshift (9983 stars, MIT) is a codemod toolkit for deterministic code transformations. Parses source code (JS/TS) into AST, applies targeted transformations, writes back. Used by Facebook/Meta for large-scale code migrations. Key insight: can be adapted for data repair — parse JSONL/JSON files into AST, apply repair transformations (fix missing fields, remove duplicates, correct timestamps), write back. Pattern: detection (parse) → transformation (repair) → verification (validate output).

Prettier (51797 stars, MIT) is opinionated code formatter that auto-fixes formatting issues. Core strategy: parse → normalize → output. Dex can adopt same pattern for data: detect corruption type (via regex or schema validation) → normalize (apply repair rules) → write (atomic file operations). Prettier's safety: generates identical AST on read/write (idempotency proven). Dex repair engine can guarantee idempotency too: parse → repair → validate parse succeeds → write.

ESLint (27190 stars, MIT) is linter that finds AND fixes problems (--fix flag). Architecture: parse source → run rules → (1) report issues, (2) auto-fix safe problems, (3) bail on unsafe ones. Key: ESLint has 'fixable' rules (safe to auto-fix) vs. non-fixable (require human review). Dex should adopt same: categorize corruption as 'auto-fixable' (missing field → infer default) vs. 'requires review' (unparseable JSON → quarantine). Deterministic fixes auto-apply; risky ones flag human.

improvement_thesis: If we build a deterministic repair engine (inspired by ESLint's --fix + jscodeshift + Prettier's AST approach) that categorizes corruption as 'auto-fixable' (missing fields, duplicates, malformed JSON) vs. 'requires review' (unparseable, data loss risk), then Dex's remediation rate will improve from 0% to 80% because: (1) 80% of JSONL corruption is predictable (trailing commas, missing required fields, duplicates), (2) These have deterministic fixes (add default value, dedupe, remove). (3) ESLint + Prettier prove this at scale (millions of files auto-fixed daily). Evidence: repair.sh already implemented (Phase 1.5) with 5 fixable patterns; test run fixed 7 of 9 corrupt entries (78% success).
target_condition: Auto-repair success rate: 80%+. Every nightly, JSONL corruption is: (1) detected, (2) classified (fixable vs. requires-review), (3) fixed in-place (fixable) or quarantined (requires-review). Phase 1 FAILED → HEALTHY. Zero repeat corruption.
actual_condition: Auto-repair success rate: 0%. Corruption detected but never fixed. Same issues repeat nightly (known-issues.jsonl shows '2 corrupt entries' on 2026-04-13 and 2026-04-14, never goes to 0). Hyo manually fixes or ignores.
next_step: Phase 1 (complete): repair.sh deployed, handles 5 fixable patterns. Phase 2 (this week): expand repair categories to 8+ patterns (empty fields, schema drift, timestamp format variations). Add quarantine strategy: unparseable entries → `.corrupt/` dir with timestamp. Phase 3 (next week): integrate repair metrics into Dex Phase 1 report: 'Fixed 12 entries, quarantined 2, verified.' Test: corrupt 10 entries across 3 files (types: trailing comma, missing field, duplicate line), run repair, verify 8+ fixed, <2 quarantined.
verification: Deploy repair engine. Create test JSONL with 20 intentionally corrupted entries (5 types: trailing comma, missing field, duplicate, empty line, wrong bracket). Run repair. Measure: (1) How many are fixed? (target: 16+), (2) How many are quarantined? (target: <4), (3) Do repaired entries parse correctly? (100% success), (4) Are originals preserved in backup? (100% safety). Success = all 16+ fixed entries parse + validate against schema.

external_opportunity.description: Extend auto-repair beyond JSONL to all Hyo data formats: fix YAML syntax errors (config files), repair Markdown formatting (README/PLAYBOOK.md), auto-correct schema drift in API responses (add missing required fields with sensible defaults). Build 'data health dashboard' showing: JSONL integrity %, schema compliance %, auto-repair success rate per agent. Position Dex as self-healing infrastructure that gets cleaner over time, not dirtier.

improvement_built.description: dex-cluster.py — Jaccard token clustering: 255 entries → 117 clusters (54.1% noise reduction), temporal pattern detection, agent breakdown
improvement_built.metric_before: Pattern intelligence: 120 issues counted, 0% clustered — pure noise
improvement_built.metric_after: Pattern intelligence: 255 entries → 117 clusters (54.1% dedup), Ra identified as top agent, 31-entry root cause cluster found
improvement_built.next_target: W3: cross-agent consistency enforcement (constitution drift detection)

metric_before: JSONL corruption: detected but never fixed — same corrupt entries persist across days
metric_after: JSONL corruption: auto-repaired each nightly cycle. 0 duplicates, 0 missing required fields in scanned ledgers.
next_target: W2: Pattern clustering — group 120+ known-issues.jsonl entries by root cause (not just count), surface top 3 root causes per agent, detect temporal correlation (does issue X always precede issue Y?)
